**How to use this file:** Snapshot of current project status across all Evryn repos. NOT a log ‚Äî each update replaces the previous state. Updated during #lock by whoever is active (AC or Soren). Read this to orient on what's happening now.

**Do not edit this file without Justin's approval.** Propose changes; don't make them directly.

**Keep this file under 50 lines.** If a project needs more than 2-3 lines, the detail belongs in that repo's own state file or build doc ‚Äî not here.

## Strategic Pivot: Evryn Product First

Team agent build (Lucas) paused ‚Äî not cancelled. Building Evryn product MVP (v0.2 "Gatekeeper's Inbox") for Mark (real-Mark = Mark Titus, Seattle filmmaker / Eva's Wild founder) first. See `evryn-backend/docs/BUILD-EVRYN-MVP.md`. Evryn is a broker, not a SaaS ‚Äî surfaces connections from a gatekeeper's inbox; everyone is a "user," both sides pay per-connection.

## How AC works now

AC drives DC and QC as **subagents** (build/review loops), not hand-relayed mailbox notes. Protocol: `docs/protocols/ac-orchestration-protocol.md`. Subagent startup loading is standardized via the **Startup Context Cascade** (verbatim "read and faithfully follow" trigger + receipts watch on the task-specific additions), with **`#cascade-override`** the only guard-railed way to narrow a load (2026-06-06). QC verifies every real code change. **AC promotes QC's standing patterns** into QC's CLAUDE.md "Patterns This Role Watches For" section (QC can't write her own; new 2026-06-03). **Ping-by-default** on `#team-alerts`. Arch + BUILD docs: AC holds edit rights, **editing needs Justin's explicit auth first**.

- **Context-architecture fixes: BUILT + QC-verified + DEPLOYED (AC1, 2026-06-08, `75fba9e`).** The live-test findings previously *routed* to Soren are now *shipped*: conversation history is runtime-owned (Evryn writes only the body; the runtime appends history once, labeled with real display-name + email, not "(user)"/UUID), the attached-history count is visible at `submit_draft` so Justin can verify it, plus a **double-send guard** (a re-approve after a successful send can't send twice) and **silent-failure alerts** (stale-check / approval-lookup / cron-checker DB errors now `notifyDev` instead of swallowing). Mira **PR #9** (identity tighten ‚Äî create-from-zero gate + context-duplicate wording) merged; `core.md` history-claim scoped to the email/submit_draft path. Reviewed by **real DC + real QC** (the earlier generic-subagent review was redone properly ‚Äî see AGENT_PATTERNS "Getting Subagents to Actually Load Their Context"). 2 minor non-blockers (drain-cooldown reset; stale collision help-string) ‚Üí SPRINT hardening list (week of 6/8).
- **Email-format + approval-flow polish: BUILT + QC-verified + DEPLOYED (AC1, 2026-06-09, `83fdd5e`).** The review/sent emails are cleaned up (review@ = classification ‚Üí draft ‚Üí context, printed *once*, newest-first; Mark's email = draft + the incoming forward only, no rolling history ‚Äî fixes the ~7√ó reprint from `stripReplyChain` being a no-op on Gmail nested quotes). Bare approvals are now **single-route** (clean "Confirmed ‚Äî sent," no hollow LLM follow-up; awareness preserved via the persisted records ‚Äî **ADR-035 ¬ß3 superseded**, amendment written). Direct-message review dedup + SDK typecheck cast (`npm run typecheck` green). Mira's 2 identity changes (`30d85f0` + Closing-the-Loop) rode the same deploy. DC built, AC+QC verified GO.
